reasoning: The Artemis Accords have acquired 55 signatories in roughly 55 months, yielding a base rate of roughly 1 new signatory per month on average. However, signings are not uniformly distributed; they heavily cluster around diplomatic summits and major international space conferences. Specifically, historically at least one country has signed the Accords every single June since their creation in 2020 (Brazil in June 2021, France in June 2022, India and Ecuador in June 2023, Armenia in June 2024). A key driver for this seasonality is the main regular session of the UN Committee on the Peaceful Uses of Outer Space (COPUOS), which typically takes place in June. The 68th session of COPUOS is scheduled to take place at the UN in Vienna from June 25 to July 4, 2025. Given the perfect historical track record of June signings, the ongoing momentum of the Accords, and the well-timed COPUOS session acting as a catalyst for formalized announcements, there is a strong structural likelihood that at least one new country will sign in June 2025. I assign a 73% probability.
